title: A Chit Chat of Discussions
wikitext:
[]: A Chit-Chat of Discussions             Quote Post by Bankin' Stein » Fri Sep 20, 2013 11:22 pm A simple enough question. What is your favorite thing to analyze/discuss about Evangelion and (if you want) why? Is it the characters? The symbolism? The psychology? The obscure technical details? Anno? EDIT: I don't see why I shouldn't share. For me it is speculation of the mental nature of the more "otherworldly" characters (Rei, the angels) as well as the possibility that the characters most accept to be humans, although still most likely human, might possess secrets from beyond the main setting's realm (I'm looking at you, Misato and your parents).

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Fri Sep 20, 2013 11:27 pm Instrumentality. WAY too many people dismiss the show's transhumanist themes as a bad thing out of hand, usually because of HOW it happened rather than because of anything specific to instrumentality itself.

[]:             Quote Post by Stillborn » Sat Sep 21, 2013 1:51 am I for one, am usually all on for transhumanizm. Bacause if you can improve in a way, that will not make you feel a lonely outcast... Why not? In Nihei's works, transhumanizm is nearly necessity to even survive in harsh enviroment characters are placed in. So yeah, I don't consider transhumanizm a bad thing. Just Evangelion and Instrumentality by SEELE and Gendo made it look bad.

[]:             Quote Post by CJD » Sat Sep 21, 2013 10:24 am I don't really get into the more heavy topics of discussion often anymore, but when I do it tends to be about the characters, their relationships, and their psychology. Specifically Asuka and Shinji since I (like to think I) understand them better than other characters. Why? I'unno, because it's stimulating I guess. I like both characters a lot, and engaging in discussions about them is a good way to both burn away the weaker theories I have and discover stronger theories to complement my own thoughts. I also enjoy discussing the more abstract psychology of the show, but I only do it rarely since that tends to devolve into me being hopelessly negative.

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Sat Sep 21, 2013 12:05 pm In no particular order, the bees in my bonnet are Asuka, anti-shipping AxS, the DC material as pointless retcon, EoE as BAD END, and NGE as illuminist/transhumanist SF, with a side of how it assumes you're as deeply into the wider genre as the original GAINAX crew. I restrain myself on the subject of "Rebuild sucks and here's why"

[]:             Quote Post by Kendrix » Sat Sep 21, 2013 2:15 pm Transhumanism got kinda used as a metaphor in EVA, rather than to discuss the subject itself; So instrumentality is kinda bad by default because of the philosophic themes it's meant to symbolize - I mean, Shinji did at least give it a try, and found that it sucked. And unlike in other settings involving transhumanism, they're using some old stuff left behind by humanity's "parents" and relying on that, unlike the normal thing where you'd have a theme of "humanity improving by its own streght" - Gendo had a bit of that going on (in contrast to SEELE), but his mistake was perhaps, to assume that something as drastic as instrumentality (that still relies on FAR artifacts) was necessary. It is, by default, stagnation instead of developing further. You could easily put a different spin on that in a fanfic, something like, "So what if humanity is a "dead end"? We got technology, thank you very much for that, dear FAR, but we won't be needing your other mojo." I think I read this one Asuka-centric fic where she ended up finding a job at adapting technology originally used for the EVAs as "complete" man-machine interfaces.

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Sat Sep 21, 2013 2:52 pm This thread isn't for discussing transhumanism. That goes http://forum.evageeks.org/thread/9200/An-Argument-for-Transhumanistic-Instrumentality/ Pay attention to the OP's question and not just the answers other people have given.

[]:             Quote Post by Fireball » Sat Sep 21, 2013 4:40 pm I love arguing with cynical fucks about Shinji.

[]:             Quote Post by Kendrix » Sat Sep 21, 2013 6:05 pm More than anything, I love the fact that this show still privides arguing material 20 years later. Sure, Rebuild added new mysteries, but we're still arguing about the original often enough.

[]:             Quote Post by pwhodges » Sat Sep 21, 2013 6:25 pm For me it's all about characters, and mostly about Shinji as the one I can most relate to. So at times I find myself trying to get my understanding of him across to people who clearly have a different view...

[]:             Quote Post by Stillborn » Sat Sep 21, 2013 7:33 pm When I was a kid, at first it was just because Evangelion was an anime. Something that was scarecely known in my country. Secondly it was the cool robots and monster fights. As i got older, I being the mere sappy mortal, started looking for a romantic relations between characters... As i grew older I started seeing Eva as a unique production with serious plots and tragedies. Eventually I reached a point where I started to see it as another "movie made known by making characters suffer", like Elfen Lied for example. It was much later I started to suspect there was some philosophy packed there in the form of anime, to make it easier to hammer it into audience's brain. By that time I started getting disgusted by it. Characters designed only for the sake to explode with filthyness of human nature. And the ending was bitter for me. Still some interest remained. In characters, in their future after EoE and even in giant robots. I clinged to fanfics that would take upon expanding or altering the universe. Often really badly written. Anything to fill the holes left in my mind by the movie. And I somehow managed to enjoy it, that even if Anno denied his creations actual happines, someones imagination may change that. No matter how inexperienced writer that person could be. Evangelion left some kind of hole in me, that no other robot shows, like Rahxephon or Gurren Lagann coud satisfy. And after years of that, I got NTE. Of course I was instantly curious mostly about the action scenes made with new technology. And I wasn't disappointed. I liked it. I liked first and second movie, despite the cheesines and shallownes of it (as many voices shouted in outrage). I liked that Shinji finally grabbed something worth fighting for, as well as was worried about Asuka, nd wished to see them all prevail. But of course it was only for Anno to make the coming blow more painful. kinda like shoving a piece of bread to a starving persons face, just inch from their grasp, and then taking it away to make the beggar suffer more. And I got a third movie. And in this... There is nothing I would cling with interest to. What I saw, was a kid, meticulously ripping wings of a fly, and tossing the now crippled fly into anthill he previously demolished. Of course ants instantly attack the intruder, but before they can tear it, the kid is burning the fly with magnifying glass, as well as the ants that were nearby. Eventually the fly is dragged into anthill to fate unknown and kid is given ovation for how artfully he tormented the insects. Glorious. Now I'm waiting for equally wonderful finale, where the wingless and burned fly, will have to pretend to be an ant, and disappear in the crowd, become invisible, lest it will be torn as intruder or food. Eventually the film will reach conclusion of how the fly realizes how wonderful it is to be an ant, despite being a crippled and burned fly unable to ever again get in the air, and will joyfully fullfil its duties as a nameless drone. The kid once again gets the ovation. I can hardly wait So yeah, Evangelion was about many things for me, changing, depending on my age. Now I'm at the point where I barely can stand it. Q managed to suck all the joy I got from Evangelion. I can't even look at fanart with happy and careless Eva characters without nagging voice in the back in my head "Anno won't allow this". And my current reason for clinging to Eva is waiting for the last movie only to get confirmation of my thesis.

[]:             Quote Post by Bankin' Stein » Sat Sep 21, 2013 7:56 pm I am also keen (and granted, this is often closer to chit-chat than discussion) on speculating how characters would react to other characters that they have little to no contact with in the series. For example, what would Yui think of Kaworu? Things like that. As long as it distances itself from what I WISH would happen from what actually would happen, it doesn't get boxed into fan-fiction.

[]:             Quote Post by user-02 » Sun Sep 22, 2013 10:40 pm I don't know if I'd call it a favorite subject, but one that I think is heavily under-discussed is the juxtaposition of the three children's roles in, and reactions to, 3II. Asuka chooses life right before her death. Shinji chooses death, but then chooses rebirth. Rei chooses to turn into a giant, naked, porcelain moon-goddess and go all Godzilla on everyone's ass. Anyhow, it's an interesting counterpoint. Lots of symbolism.

[]:             Quote Post by C.A.P. » Mon Sep 23, 2013 12:03 am Figuring out what the heck happened to Gainax at that time.

[]:             Quote Post by Dream » Mon Sep 23, 2013 3:19 am A few years ago, i would have said things about the characters and their interpersonal relationship. Nowadays i'm more interested in the hollistic aspects or macro-cosmic (lol) areas of Evangelion. What the story in the whole is saying, the roles the characters fulfill in Anno's message, the usage of narrative elements to create a meta-aesthetic, things like that. The author/creation dynamic is also a favorite of mine. Also, this: C.A.P. wrote: Figuring out what the heck happened to Gainax at that time.

[]:             Quote Post by Monk Ed » Mon Sep 23, 2013 4:55 am There's no single aspect I could possibly name. From impact mechanics, to relationship analysis, to shipping/anti-shipping debates, there's very few topics under the umbrella of NGE Discussion I don't like. I don't like them all equally all the time, but I've had my passions struck with just about each of them at some time or another, enough to put hours of my day for days in a row into reading and/or posting. The Discussion forum, when it's sufficiently active, is and always has been my favorite place on the whole site. It's no wonder, then, that I was assigned as its primary steward among the three of us new mods (although it hasn't been nearly as active as the Rebuild forum during my tenure thus far so that is where most of my time and energy as moderator and thread surgeon gets spent).

[]:             Quote Post by C.A.P. » Mon Sep 23, 2013 8:38 am Dream wrote: Hideaki Anno has been an extremely fascinating person and artist to me, and the story behind the making of of evangelion has always been quite an appasionating thing to learn about.

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Mon Sep 23, 2013 9:38 am NemZ wrote: Instrumentality. WAY too many people dismiss the show's transhumanist themes as a bad thing out of hand, usually because of HOW it happened rather than because of anything specific to instrumentality itself.

[]:             Quote Post by Rei IV » Mon Sep 23, 2013 2:38 pm Tines and NemZ's distinct views regarding EoTV and EoE, which have more than ever convinced me there is a basis for what the (* personally ) believe and that EoTV is in fact, GOOD END. I struggle with my overall personal feelings with EoE. On one had, I like the movie and on the other, part me wants to hate it and dismiss as a nihilistic POS, like Anno! I also like the heated discussions about Asuka's character because Bagheera will come to the rescue and intervene, giving a thesis as to why she does NOT suck and how she's NOT a evil bitch and how our issues with the character are our OWN fault. I admire the patience, effort, and scholarly approach he puts in to defending character and in his depth, some what occasionally lengthy posts regarding her character. It makes me envious because if someone told me or said that Rei sucks, Rei is an emotionless doll, Rei is just fussy about who pulls her strings, LRS is incest and perverse, LRS fans are perverts, I'd probably just sit there like a wimp with a limp, feeble pathetic "It's okay! I respect your opinion even if I don't agree with it!".

[]:             Quote Post by Singularity84 » Mon Sep 23, 2013 5:04 pm Its always been little details for me. I love going back to the series and movies and noticing details and symbolism I had missed. Of course I don't tend to discuss this with my friends that often, so as I don't look pretentious in my views on a "cartoon." Also End of Eva is in a category of it's own in terms of theatrical works for me. Not only is it what I immediately turn to if I want to show my friends something really fucked up, its also one of the few movies I can consider to be art. Anything surreal or difficult to comprehend instantly appeals to me, with Eva being no exception.
